mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, sustained restrictive policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, reducing present values of future corporate cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49 as of t-1mo near restrictive threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based demand expansion that amplifies corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns, with Rank IC below 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy for longer, raising the equity risk premium and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases during tightening cycles coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A reading of housing starts (HOUST) below 2000 thousand units reflects a contraction in residential investment and broader demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2276 as of t-2mo but vulnerable to decline
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 2000, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of month-over-month rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a federal funds rate above 4% indicates stagflationary pressure where monetary policy is constrained from easing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of FEDFUNDS > 4% and rising CPIAUCSL produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% reflects a tight labor market that pressures corporate profit margins through wage costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as margin compression dominates revenue growth.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.
